seo.title: Monitoring & Observability implementation checklist
seo.description: A practical checklist for shipping Monitoring & Observability projects safely.

content.intro: This checklist outlines the technical requirements for establishing a robust observability stack, covering error tracking, LLM performance monitoring, infrastructure metrics, and alerting workflows to ensure production reliability and cost control.

items.title: Global Exception Handler Integration
items.description: Configure a global error handler (e.g., Sentry, Highlight.io) at the application entry point to capture unhandled exceptions and promise rejections.

items.title: Source Map Upload Pipeline
items.description: Integrate source map uploads into the CI/CD pipeline to ensure stack traces map back to original source code rather than minified bundles.

items.title: User Context Enrichment
items.description: Attach non-PII user identifiers and session IDs to error reports to facilitate reproduction and impact assessment of user-specific bugs.

items.title: Release Tracking Configuration
items.description: Tag every error with a specific commit SHA or version number to identify regressions introduced in specific deployments.

items.title: Breadcrumb Logging Implementation
items.description: Configure automatic capture of network requests, console logs, and state changes leading up to a crash for debugging context.

items.title: Token Usage Instrumentation
items.description: Implement middleware (e.g., Helicone, LangSmith) to log prompt and completion tokens for every LLM provider call to track unit costs.

items.title: Provider Latency Measurement
items.description: Measure Time-To-First-Token (TTFT) and total request duration for all LLM API calls to identify provider-side performance degradation.

items.title: Model Version Tagging
items.description: Explicitly tag metrics with specific model versions (e.g., gpt-4-0613 vs gpt-4-turbo) to evaluate performance and cost deltas.

items.title: Request/Response Payload Logging
items.description: Securely log prompt inputs and model outputs for a sampled percentage of requests to monitor for hallucinations or drift.

items.title: Semantic Search Latency Tracking
items.description: Monitor the latency of vector database queries and embedding generation steps in RAG pipelines.

items.title: Node/Instance Resource Alerts
items.description: Set up Prometheus or Datadog alerts for CPU utilization >85% and Memory utilization >90% sustained for over 5 minutes.

items.title: HTTP 5xx Error Rate Thresholds
items.description: Configure alerts for server-side error rates exceeding 1% of total traffic over a 60-second window.

items.title: Database Connection Pool Monitoring
items.description: Monitor active vs. maximum available database connections to prevent application hangs during traffic spikes.

items.title: Disk I/O and Space Monitoring
items.description: Establish alerts for disk space usage exceeding 80% and high I/O wait times on database volumes.

items.title: Queue Depth and Lag Tracking
items.description: For worker-based systems, monitor the number of pending messages and the time-to-process for background jobs.

items.title: Trace ID Propagation
items.description: Ensure trace headers (e.g., W3C Trace Context) are passed across all service boundaries, including internal microservices and external proxies.

items.title: Database Query Instrumentation
items.description: Enable auto-instrumentation for database drivers to capture slow queries as spans within a distributed trace.

items.title: Sampling Rate Calibration
items.description: Configure trace sampling rates (e.g., 10% for high-volume, 100% for errors) to balance visibility with storage costs.

items.title: External API Dependency Mapping
items.description: Instrument all outgoing HTTP calls to third-party services to identify which external dependency is causing latency.

items.title: Async Task Trace Linking
items.description: Ensure that background jobs inherit the trace context from the triggering HTTP request for end-to-end visibility.

items.title: External Uptime Heartbeats
items.description: Configure external probes (e.g., BetterStack) to check the /health endpoint from multiple geographic regions every 60 seconds.

items.title: On-Call Escalation Policy
items.description: Define a clear escalation path in an incident management tool to ensure critical alerts page a human responder within 5 minutes.

items.title: Alert Severity Categorization
items.description: Distinguish between 'Critical' (page responder) and 'Warning' (Slack notification only) to prevent alert fatigue.

items.title: Synthetic Transaction Monitoring
items.description: Script a critical user path (e.g., login or checkout) to run every 5-15 minutes to verify functional correctness.

items.title: Post-Mortem Documentation Template
items.description: Establish a standardized template for documenting root causes and action items after every high-severity incident.
